title: polypodium baudouinii
intro: Polypodium baudouinii, also known as the Baudouin's polypody, is a species of fern native to tropical Africa, specifically in the countries of Cameroon, Republic of the Congo, and Democratic Republic of the Congo. The plant is known for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant properties. The health benefits of Polypodium baudouinii include its ability to treat various health issues, such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. Herbal preparations of Polypodium baudouinii are made by infusing the plant's leaves in water or ethanol to create teas, tinctures, and infusions that can be used to treat various health conditions.
